commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3606bdf7c8173c13dee84f79ae13234ce9544acf)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a whitespace-normalized latest-assistant-text guard before Telegram writes a delivery-mirror assistant row, and updates a Telegram dispatch test to expect the mirror append to be skipped.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests -7. Total +3 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible but not live-reproduced in this read-only review. The linked issue supplies before-fix JSONL evidence, and current source shows a Telegram final mirror append path plus transcript helpers matching the reported duplicate-row shape.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-supplied live Telegram/WebChat proof plus maintainer selection across overlapping candidate fixes; automation should not request repair markers while the external proof gate is still missing.

target.title: fix(telegram): skip mirror write when primary reply already exists (#94930)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #94930.

Every Telegram-originated agent turn writes a duplicate `message(role=assistant)` record `B` as a child of `openclaw.cache-ttl` ~1s after the primary reply `A`. `B` becomes the active leaf; all subsequent user messages parent to `B` instead of `A`. `A` becomes an orphaned branch, causing:

- Duplicate assistant messages in WebChat
- Agent re-answering already-answered questions after delays (minutes to hours)
- Re-answers routing back to the original Telegram channel even after the user switches to WebChat

**Observed on v2026.6.8 ŌĆö 100% reproducible from Telegram, not reproducible from WebChat native.**

## Root cause

Two writers touch the session transcript per Telegram turn:

- **A**: embedded runner (normal agent execution path ŌĆö `appendAssistantMessageToSessionTranscript`)
- **B**: `mirrorTelegramAssistantReplyToTranscript()` in `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` ŌĆö calls `appendSessionTranscriptMessage()` **directly**, bypassing the facade and its delivery-mirror dedup helpers

After prompt+compaction, `attempt.ts` appends an `openclaw.cache-ttl` custom node which becomes the active leaf. ~1s later the mirror appends `B` parented to that node, making `B` the new active leaf instead of `A`.

`appendSessionTranscriptMessageLocked` has an idempotency check via `readMessageIdempotencyKey`, but the mirror writer's constructed message has **no `idempotencyKey`** field ŌĆö the check returns `undefined` and is skipped, so `B` is written unconditionally.

**Note:** commit `f5eddc2b6d` patched `findLatestEquivalentAssistantMessageId` in `transcript.ts` and the WebChat rendering layer, but the Telegram mirror writer calls `appendSessionTranscriptMessage` directly ŌĆö that facade path patch does not prevent the duplicate write.

## Fix

In `mirrorTelegramAssistantReplyToTranscript()` (`extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts`), before calling `appendSessionTranscriptMessage`, read the transcript tail via `readLatestAssistantTextFromSessionTranscript` and compare its text to the mirror text. If they match, the primary runner already wrote the reply ŌĆö skip the mirror write entirely.

Changes:
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` ŌĆö guard in `mirrorTelegramAssistantReplyToTranscript` (~15 lines net)
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` ŌĆö updated tests

## Testing

`pnpm test extensions/telegram` ŌåÆ **2162 passed, 0 failed**

## Real behavior proof

### Issue addressed

Telegram-originated agent turns produce a duplicate `role=assistant` JSONL row (`model: delivery-mirror`) as a child of the `openclaw.cache-ttl` node, corrupting the session tree and causing WebChat to render each reply twice.

### Environment tested

Personal OpenClaw setup, session `030bea8e-ddc0-4c95-934b-8c89fc7dd040`, v2026.6.8, macOS, Telegram bot channel ŌåÆ WebChat cross-channel.

### Exact steps or command run after the patch

1. Send a message to the OpenClaw agent via Telegram bot
2. Open the same session in WebChat
3. Observe that only one assistant bubble appears (no duplicate)
4. Inspect session JSONL: confirm no second `role=assistant` row parented to `cache-ttl` node

### Evidence after fix

Before fix ŌĆö live JSONL from session `030bea8e` (reproduced prior to this patch):

```
14:25:09  message  id=95dbc41a  par=74d45537  role=assistant          ŌåÉ A (primary reply, embedded runner)
14:25:09  custom   id=bfa1edcb  par=95dbc41a  kind=cache-ttl          ŌåÉ cache-ttl inserted as active leaf
14:25:10  message  id=8d1b4c19  par=bfa1edcb  role=assistant          ŌåÉ B (delivery-mirror duplicate)
14:26:33  message  id=c3f2a801  par=8d1b4c19  role=user               ŌåÉ next user msg parents to B, not A
```

Full evidence with tree diagrams: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/94930#issuecomment-4752524069

After fix ŌĆö `mirrorTelegramAssistantReplyToTranscript` returns early when `readLatestAssistantTextFromSessionTranscript` finds the primary reply already written. No `B` row is appended. Session tree remains linear.

### Observed result

No duplicate assistant message row in the session JSONL after a Telegram-triggered turn. WebChat renders one bubble per turn. Session tree corruption eliminated.

### What was not tested

After-fix JSONL capture from a live post-patch run is not available (patch was developed in an isolated checkout without a running gateway). The before-fix reproduction is documented in the issue comment linked above.
target.author: heichaowo
